name: hotfix-watcher
description: Watch selected GitHub PRs until merged, run a configurable hotfix CLI, then optionally dispatch the matching pre/prod deploy workflow(s).

fordefiHotfix.owner.description: GitHub repository owner used for PR search and API calls.

fordefiHotfix.recentPrCount.description: How many of your most recently updated PRs to fetch from GitHub.

fordefiHotfix.displayPrLimit.description: Maximum number of PR rows shown in the sidebar at once. Checked PRs always stay visible even when they fall below this limit.

fordefiHotfix.pollIntervalSeconds.description: Seconds between merge checks while watching.

fordefiHotfix.repoRoot.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the git clone where the wrapper script your `commandTemplate` invokes (e.g. `./fcli`) lives. Empty falls back to the first workspace folder.

fordefiHotfix.worktreeSshKey.markdownDescription: Optional path to an SSH private key used **only inside the hotfix worktree** (e.g. `~/.ssh/id_ed25519_sk_notouch`). Empty leaves git/ssh untouched. When set, the extension scopes `core.sshCommand` to the worktree via `extensions.worktreeConfig` + `git config --worktree` so `git pull/fetch/push` inside `<repoRoot>-hotfix-worktree` skips the YubiKey tap, while the parent repo and any other clone keep using their normal (touch-required) key. Typical use: generate a no-touch FIDO key (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519-sk -O no-touch-required -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_sk_notouch`) and add it as a deploy/login key on GitHub.

fordefiHotfix.commandTemplate.markdownDescription:
Shell command run after every selected PR is merged. Must include `{prNumbers}`.

Placeholders:
- `{repoRoot}` — value of **Repo root**
- `{prNumbers}` — space-separated PR numbers, ascending
- `{prList}` — comma-separated PR numbers
- `{owner}` / `{repo}` — from **Owner** / **Repo**
- `{hotfixSuffix}` — fcli flags built from the **Hotfix CLI** row (env, draft, critical fast track)

fordefiHotfix.debugTerminal.markdownDescription: When **off** (default) the hotfix command runs in **transparent mode**: no terminal panel opens, output goes to the *Fordefi Hotfix CLI* output channel silently, and the user only sees notifications for actions (YubiKey touch, merge conflict, fatal error) and milestones (hotfix PR created, deploy started/finished). When **on**, the command runs in a visible integrated terminal — the legacy debug flow — so you can watch every line, intervene by hand, and answer prompts directly. Use the **Hotfix: Toggle debug terminal mode** command or the status-bar item to flip this on the fly.

fordefiHotfix.hotfixRunMode.markdownDescription:
**Deprecated — prefer `fordefiHotfix.debugTerminal` instead.** How to run the hotfix command after merges.

- **transparent** (default) — silent spawn; user is only notified for actions / milestones.
- **integratedTerminal** — real terminal you can interact with (debug mode).
- **background** — legacy spawn without prompt detection; treated as `transparent` going forward.

When `debugTerminal` is `true` the run always uses `integratedTerminal` regardless of this setting.

fordefiHotfix.hotfixTerminalName.markdownDescription: Integrated terminal tab name when **Run mode** is `integratedTerminal`.

fordefiHotfix.hotfixEnv.markdownDescription: Default value for the **env** dropdown in the Hotfix CLI row. Forwarded to fcli as `--env`. Choose **both** to pass `--env pre --env prod` (fcli accepts repeated `-e`).

fordefiHotfix.hotfixDraft.markdownDescription: Default state of the **draft** checkbox. When on, passes `--draft` to fcli (fcli default: `--no-draft`).

fordefiHotfix.hotfixCriticalFastTrack.markdownDescription: Default state of the **critical fast track** checkbox. When on, passes `--critical-fast-track` to fcli (skips E2E in CI per fcli help).

fordefiHotfix.hotfixDeploy.markdownDescription: Default state of the **deploy** checkbox. When on, after the fcli-created hotfix PR is merged the extension dispatches the matching workflow(s) in `arnac-io/workflows` (`pre-hotfix.yml`, `production-hotfix.yml`, or pre → wait for success → prod).

fordefiHotfix.workflowsOwner.description: Owner of the repository that hosts the hotfix GitHub Actions workflows.

fordefiHotfix.workflowsRepo.markdownDescription: Repository that hosts the hotfix GitHub Actions workflows (dispatched via `gh workflow run`).

fordefiHotfix.preHotfixWorkflow.description: Workflow file name for the pre-environment hotfix deploy.

fordefiHotfix.productionHotfixWorkflow.description: Workflow file name for the production-environment hotfix deploy.

fordefiHotfix.workflowRef.markdownDescription: Git ref passed to `gh workflow run --ref` when dispatching hotfix workflows.

fordefiHotfix.hotfixTerminalAutoFirstCon.markdownDescription: Integrated terminal only: after the hotfix command starts (shell integration), send **Auto first confirm text** once after the configured delay. Useful when fcli has no `--yes` but still asks for the first `y`/confirmation; per-environment YubiKey prompts are unaffected. Best-effort — turn off if it double-submits or fires too early.
Keystrokes sent for the first prompt (e.g. `y` or `yes`). If the value contains no line break, Enter is appended automatically.
Milliseconds to wait after the command starts before sending **Auto first confirm text**. Increase if the prompt appears late.

fordefiHotfix.githubPat.markdownDescription: Optional PAT override, only useful when `gh auth token` is unavailable. Prefer `gh auth login`.

fordefiHotfix.ghPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `gh` executable (e.g. `/opt/homebrew/bin/gh`). Empty searches `PATH` — required on macOS if VS Code/Cursor was not started from a shell.

fordefiHotfix.worktreePostCreateCommand.markdownDescription: Shell command run **once**, in the hotfix worktree's cwd, immediately after `git worktree add` succeeds. Skipped on reuse. Default `./atool prepare-codeenv` regenerates the python codeenv so fcli doesn't crash on stale protobufs (e.g. `AttributeError: module ... has no attribute 'BytesEqualsArgumentCondition'`). Empty disables the step. Failures are logged but do not block the run.

scripts.test:e2e: npm run compile && npm run test:e2e:compile && node test/e2e/out/runTest.js
scripts.verify: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run compile && npm run typecheck:test && npm run test
